question_text: A magnetic force of 12 N pulls a piece of iron towards a magnet. There are no other horizontal forces acting. What is the net force on the iron?
explanation: Net force is the overall effect of all forces combined. Since the magnetic force is the only horizontal force acting, the net force equals the magnetic force itself: 12 N in the direction of the magnet. This will cause the iron to accelerate towards the magnet.
